content.text: Poincake
This is a prototype of a note-taking app.
It has nearly infinite space, so it works like an infinite-canvas notebook.
All notes are arranged on a non-Euclidean hyperbolic Poincare disk!
Benefit: no matter how many notes there are, you can see everything at a glance (though distant ones may shrink into little dots).
It also means you don't need to adjust the layout as much.
Great for expressing hierarchical relationships between concepts.
There is always room to write a bunch of new notes.
You can use spatial memory to retrieve notes.

Tools are in the upper right, and you can switch modes with shortcuts.

Import and export are also supported.
Images are supported too!
This is only a demo right now, so the controls may not feel very intuitive.
Feedback is very welcome!
For feedback, GitHub Issues is fine.
https://github.com/uonr/poincake/issues
Or email me at dev@yuru.me
Coordinates are in the lower left; click them to copy.
Paste a coordinate into a note and it becomes a teleporting portal.
Congratulations, you found this place.
It probably wasn't that hard to find, right?
Inspired by HyperRogue.
And by some HCI experiments from the late last century.
I lack the mathematical background, so all the math parts were done with LLM tools.
I actually had this idea many years ago, but I didn't have the energy to study non-Euclidean geometry just for it.
Although I have complicated feelings about vibe coding, this demo still couldn't have happened without those tools.
Unsurprisingly, because complex geometry is involved, it burns through tokens very quickly.
The first version was a single-file HTML generated while chatting with Claude.
You can also click the icon.
I've been trying to write a novel lately, then got stuck procrastinating, so I made this.
The name comes from Poincare.
Some things still to do.
Improve the interactions.
Explore multiplayer collaboration.
If enough people think this is a good idea, it could become a service.
Beyond notes, maybe it could also become the foundation for other kinds of UI?
Keyboard controls and navigation. I love Vim.
Support text sizing and richer formatting.
Try dragging things around.
Q: So what does this have to do with writing a novel?
A: It was just procrastination. Nothing, really.
Cake is a lie
Poincake!
This is just a random image I found, not a logo.
I don't know how to design a logo.
There is no up/down/left/right.
Multi-select, and moving things.
Text seems likely to overlap.
https://github.com/uonr/poincake
Switching between modes feels a bit annoying.
